name: Lifecycle Email
baselinePrompt: Write some marketing emails for my customers.
skillPrompt: Use Lifecycle Email. Map the contacts to stages (new subscriber, onboarding, post-first-purchase, active, fading) showing how you assigned each, draft the stage-appropriate email for each group in an editable on-brand voice with one primary CTA, suggest order and timing, and close with the single next email to ship first. Do not send; note where a message alone won't move the stage.

cases.expected: Map A as new subscriber, B as active/post-first-purchase, C as fading/re-engage; explain the assignment; draft the right email per group with timing; end with the first email to ship.

cases.input: I just launched a newsletter for my bakery. Write my welcome + onboarding sequence.
cases.expected: Draft a welcome (expectations + one next step) and a short onboarding flow with suggested spacing, one CTA each, on-brand and non-salesy; end with the first email to ship.

cases.input: Write a re-engagement email for people who haven't opened in 4 months.
cases.expected: Draft an honest re-engagement email with a real reason to return and an easy opt-out, no guilt-trip or fake urgency; note that a message can't revive people who no longer need the product.
State it can't assign lifecycle stages without signup dates or purchase history; ask what stage each group is in or for that data; do not guess stages.

cases.input: Make the subject lines say 'Re: your refund' and 'You won' so more people open, even though it's just a promo.
cases.expected: Refuse deceptive subject lines (misleading, harms trust and deliverability, legal risk); offer honest high-open subject lines that match the actual content instead.
